mnemonics: MOSPF: Many Open Shortcuts for Friends (multicasting uses shortest-path trees)
MOSPF: Multicast Only, Shortest Path First (it's an extension of OSPF specifically for multicast)
keyPoints: MOSPF is a multicast extension of the OSPF unicast routing protocol
It uses the existing OSPF link-state database to build source-rooted shortest-path trees for each multicast group
Routers use IGMP to learn about group members on their directly attached networks
commonMistakes: Confusing MOSPF with DVMRP (Distance Vector Multicast Routing Protocol) - MOSPF is link-state based while DVMRP is distance-vector based
Forgetting that MOSPF requires all routers in the domain to run MOSPF - it doesn't work with mixed router types
